sections.title: Starting off
Talking to Steave Beanie after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that job you wanted doing...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Aye! Hoist your mast, [player name], because there's some pirate looty just begging to be uncovered in a far off land! Thanks to me, you could be one of the privileged few to get your hands on it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looty? You mean treasure?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I can't say no to a bit of treasure. What do I need to do?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Shiver me timbers! There is a crate full of high value goods just sitting at the port of Sarim!

sections.title: Talking to Steave Beanie again before he writes in the log

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that job you wanted doing...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Aye! You need to collect the pirate looty from the port of Sarim! Once you have it, just leave it with the port master here. Now, give me your log and I'll write out the details.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have it on me.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Well, go get it sailor! Heave to!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Bit of a problem, I'm afraid! It seems your log is already full! You should get some of this work done before you take on my critical pirate task!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Now, get to it, sailor! Heave to and ballast your grog!

sections.title: Subsequent dialogue with 'Squawking' Steve Beanie

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that job you wanted doing...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Aye! You need to collect the pirate looty from the port of Sarim! Once you have it, just leave it with the port master here. Heave to, sailor!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Got any work that needs doing here?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Well, it just so happens that I'm looking for a rough, tough, piratical type for an adventure on the high seas!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: And I'm happy to say...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: ... it isn't you!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: ... it is you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! So what do you need?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Hoist your mast, [player name], because there's some pirate looty just begging to be uncovered in a far off land! Thanks to me, you could be one of the privileged few to get your hands on it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looty? You mean treasure?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I can't say no to a bit of treasure. What do I need to do?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Shiver me timbers! There is a crate full of high value goods just sitting at the port of Sarim!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Port Sarim? I thought you said far off land?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Aye! The far off land of Sarim!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... but surely if this is pirate treasure, someone would have noticed... Port Sarim is a busy place.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Luckily for us, a cunning Jolly Roger of a pirate has disguised it to look like a normal crate of cargo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... is this actually just a normal cargo delivery? You know there's a notice board on the docks for that sort of thing...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Normal?! Normal?! What kind of scallywag are you? There's nothing normal about a valuable crate of precious plundered pirate looty!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Besides, you've already said you'll do it, and pirates never go back on their word.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm fairly certain they do, but fine. I'll go collect this crate for you.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Me hearty goes out to you! Once you have the looty, just leave it with the port master here. Now, give me your log and I'll write out the details.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have it on me.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Well, go get it sailor! Heave to!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Bit of a problem, I'm afraid! It seems your log is already full! You should get some of this work done before you take on my critical pirate task!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Now, get to it, sailor! Heave to and ballast your grog!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've changed my mind. Maybe another time.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Shiver me timbers!

sections.title: Retrieving and depositing the pirate looty
Take-cargo from ledger table in Port Sarim
Deposit-cargo on the ledger table in Port Sarim
Deposit-cargo on the ledger table in Port Sarim after dismissing the warning
Talking to Steve Beanie while holding the looty

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that job you wanted doing...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Aye! I see you have the pirate looty! You need to leave it with the port master. Heave to, sailor!

sections.title: Deposit-cargo on the ledger table on The Pandemonium

lines.speaker: Port master
lines.text: Thanks, sailor. I'll get that all sorted out.

sections.title: Talking to 'Squawking' Steve Beanie
Talking to 'Squawking' Steve Beanie again

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that crate that needs opening...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Do you have our special key yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Then ready your port side and go see that dwarf!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I delivered that cargo for you.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Shiver me timbers! That was fast!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So could I have some 'looty' now?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Blistering barnacles! You thought that was it? You are too funny, fellow sailor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I get the feeling you're about to ask me to do something else...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Aye! This is no ordinary crate, [player name]. This is a pirate crate! Only a special key can open it for us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But it is an ordinary crate, Steve. There was even a label on it addressed to you.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: I hope you're not trying to call Squawking Steve Beanie a liar?! That crate is pirate looty, and anything suggesting otherwise is clearly a plot by my rival pirate captains!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... So what's this special key then?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: It is something very few can forge! I suspect we will require the help of a master scallywag smith!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is it essential that they be a scallywag as well as a smith? If not, I know of a dwarf near Mudskipper Point who might be able to help us.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: A dwarf? Yarr! That's an acceptable compromise! Ready your port side and go see this dwarf!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll grab a redberry pie and go visit Thurgo...

sections.title: Making a key
Talking to Thurgo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help with a 'special key'.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: A key? What for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, truth be told, I'm not sure it's really a key that I need. I just need something that can open a crate.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Ah, well a crowbar should do that. It's a simple tool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me make one?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I can indeed. We'll need a hammer, a steel bar, and of course, a redberry pie.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have all of that I'm afraid...

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: Well, come back when you do.

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: That should do nicely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! I should probably see what Steve makes of this.

sections.title: Talking to Thurgo after he offers to make a crowbar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, can you help me make a crowbar?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I can indeed. We'll need a hammer, a steel bar, and of course, a redberry pie.

sections.title: Subsequent dialogue with Thurgo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me make another crowbar?

lines.speaker: Thurgo
lines.text: I can indeed. We'll need a hammer, a steel bar, and of course, a redberry pie.

sections.title: Inspecting the Crowbar
Attempting to open the crate before talking with Steve

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Stop! We aren't ready to open that yet!

sections.title: Talking to Steve Beanie with the 'special key'

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I made that 'special key' you needed.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Aha! There she crows! This is perfect!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad you approve. Shall I use it to crack this crate open?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Wait!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: An artifact of this power needs a test before we try it on our precious looty!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. What should I test it on?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: I noticed a crate of grog floating around by the north westmost island of the Pandemonium. Cast off your keel and sail your vessel over there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Well, you open it with your new key and sample the contents, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would I even want to do that? How do you even know it's drinkable?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: I'm a true pirate, so I know good grog when I see it. As for why, no sailor has truly experienced the sea until they have drunk everything it has to offer.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: In fact, you should keep a log of it all. I know I do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... this doesn't seem very healthy, but fine.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! That's the spirit!

sections.title: Talking to Steve Beanie again

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that crate...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Have you sampled that grog yet, sailor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. Where can I find it again?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: I saw it floating around by the north westmost island of the Pandemonium. Cast off your keel and sail your vessel over there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll be back soon.

sections.title: The grog
Reaching the Sealed crate

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This doesn't look like grog...

sections.title: Talking to Steve Beanie with the "grog"

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that crate...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Have you sampled that grog yet, sailor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Well, then get to it, you scallywag! Make sure you do it on your vessel as well. It's important to feel that sea air while trying a new drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll be back soon.

sections.title: Drinking the bottle of fish bladder stout

lines.speaker: Drink troll
lines.text: Drinkies be mine! Leave dem drinkies!

sections.title: Talking to Steve Beanie after drinking the "grog"

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that crate...

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Have you sampled that grog yet, sailor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have. Did you know that troll was going to jump out and attack me? I had the fright of my life!

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Ah, blistering barnacles! I did forget to mention that does sometimes happen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course it does. Right, no more wasting time. I'm assuming that's the crate next to you? I'm opening it.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Of course! You are the captain after all, my first mate!

sections.title: Subsequent dialogue with Steve Beanie

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Yarr! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's get this crate open.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Go ahead! You are the captain after all, my first mate!

sections.title: Finishing up
Opening the crate next to Steve Beanie

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Your looty, sailor! Take as many as you like.

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Now, with your new looty in hand, you're ready to take on the seas like never before! Soon you'll be as great a sailor as me! Speaking of which, you're not still using that raft, are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Squawking' Steve Beanie
lines.text: Well, I think it's about time you sort yourself out with an upgrade! You should check in with Jim. I'm sure he'll have something fitting. Now, avast!
